Title: MYB transcription factors that colour our fruit

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates anthocyanin biosynthesis and determines fruit colour in skin, flesh and foliage.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Distinct clade of R2R3 MYB transcription factors is described as controlling fruit anthocyanin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Anthocyanin concentration is a primary determinant of plant colour. 2. Fruit anthocyanin biosynthesis is controlled by a distinct clade of R2R3 MYB transcription factors. 3. In apple, recent papers identified MYB genes activating skin, flesh and foliage anthocyanic colour.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful targets for breeding and biotechnology to develop fruit with novel colour patterns through manipulation of anthocyanin accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Paper is a summary/review of discovery papers; no direct validation experiments reported in this text.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Associated with coloured skin, flesh and foliage tissues
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Associated with coloured skin, flesh and foliage tissues
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is a review/highlight rather than a primary research article; specific apple MYB gene names are not provided in the supplied text, although the article states that three recent papers describe MYB genes controlling skin, flesh and foliage colour.
